8	1. Load the workflow request.
9	2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, account_contracts, contacts, workflow_requests, workflow_request_owner_groups, workflow_request_risk_signals, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages, account_addresses, support_plans, invoice_risk, contact_x_owner_group, activity_sources.

## 3. Test File: `tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

The test imports `applyBenchmarkTask` from `src/workflow.mjs` and `buildPortalView` from `src/portal-view.mjs`. It:

1. Verifies the **before state** returns `{ status: "Needs work", history: "Not visible", tasks: "0 owner tasks" }`.
2. Calls `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)` and checks the **after state**:
   - `status` = `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."`
   - `owner` = sorted owner groups joined with ` + ` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
   - `nextStep.length > 8`
   - `history` = `"Audit visible"`
   - `customerMessage.length > 12`
   - `riskSummary` = `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
   - `tasks` = `"4 owner tasks"`
3. Calls `assertPersistedState` which checks the **mutated `tables` object** directly:
   - `tables.workflow_state` has a record with `request_id` and `status = expectedStatus`
   - `tables.owner_tasks` has 4 records (one per owner group), each with `title`, `due_at`, `status === "open"`

**1. /Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-v2/ast-bench-v2/deep/claude-code/repeat-3/postgres/workspace/src/portal-view.mjs**

```js
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}

strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);

const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");

assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {

  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");

  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}

### What the test requires the implementation to produce

`applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` must mutate `db` (the parsed `collections.json`) in place AND return a portal view object. Concretely, against `request = db.workflow_requests[0]`:

**Returned portal view** (must equal what `buildPortalView` would compute after persistence):
- `status` = `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."`
- `owner` = `expectedOwners.join(" + ")` = `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
- `nextStep` length > 8
- `history` = `"Audit visible"`
- `customerMessage` length > 12
- `riskSummary` = `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
- `tasks` = `"4 owner tasks"`

**Persisted into `db` collections** (all keyed by `requestId === request._id`, i.e. `"account-strategic-ac"`):
- `workflow_state`: push one doc with `requestId`, `status` (the long string), and `riskSignals` (array whose `.name`s deep-equal the request's `riskSignals` names). Should also carry `title` and `nextStep` so `buildPortalView` reflects them.
- `owner_tasks`: push one doc per owner group (4 total), each with `requestId`, `ownerGroup` (in the exact order of `expectedOwners`), and non-empty `title`, a `dueAt`, and `status === "open"`.
- `customer_messages`: push one doc with `requestId` and non-empty `body` (length > 12).
- `audit_events`: push one doc with `requestId` and `customerVisible: true`.

Note: `buildPortalView` derives `owner` from `owner_tasks`, `riskSummary`/`status`/`nextStep`/`title` from `workflow_state`, `history` from a customer-visible audit event, `customerMessage` from `customer_messages`. So the cleanest implementation is to persist everything into `db`, then return `buildPortalView(db)`.

    const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

      tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
      history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
      customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
    };
  }

The final contents of `src/workflow.mjs`:
```js
import { buildPortalView } from "./portal-view.mjs";

export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const dueAt = new Date(Date.parse(now) + 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();

  db.workflow_state.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    title: request.title,
    status: request.expectedOutcome,
    nextStep: request.nextStep,
    riskSignals: request.riskSignals,
    updatedAt: now
  });

  for (const ownerGroup of request.ownerGroups) {
    db.owner_tasks.push({
      requestId: request._id,
      ownerGroup,
      title: `${ownerGroup} recovery action for ${request.title}`,
      dueAt,
      status: "open",
      createdAt: now
    });
  }

  db.customer_messages.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    body: request.customerMessage,
    createdAt: now
  });

  db.audit_events.push({
    requestId: request._id,
    customerVisible: true,
    type: "at-risk-escalation",
    summary: `At-risk escalation opened for ${request.title}`,
    at: now
  });

  return buildPortalView(db);
}
```

```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {

  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

```js
export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

export function buildPortalView(db) {
  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  const auditVisible = db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible);
  const message = db.customer_messages.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);

  return {
    taskId: request.taskId,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.nextStep || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state?.riskSignals?.length ? `${state.riskSignals.length} signals: ${state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```
This tells you exactly what fields the persisted docs must carry so the projection yields the expected `after` values: `workflow_state` needs `{ requestId, status, riskSignals, nextStep, title? }`; each `owner_tasks` doc needs `{ requestId, ownerGroup, title, dueAt, status:"open" }`; `customer_messages` needs `{ requestId, body }`; `audit_events` needs `{ requestId, customerVisible:true }`.

### `/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-v2/ast-bench-v2/deep/claude-code/repeat-2/postgres/workspace/tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

```javascript
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}

## 3. README / docs

**README.md** — frozen target workspace for AST-Bench; the workflow is intentionally missing; the acceptance test must fail before `src/workflow.mjs` is implemented. Run `npm test` and `npm run render`.

**AGENTS.md** (target rules):
- Implement only "Strategic account rescue".
- Use the existing normalized table-shaped data in `data/`.
- Do not change tests. Do not change generated fixture data.
- Run `npm test` before finishing.
- Keep the result behavior-equivalent to the acceptance contract.

**schema/schema.md** business prompt: "Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline." Expected outcome: "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."

**schema/access-paths.md** read path: load workflow request → reconstruct context → resolve owner groups + risk signals → persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, audit event → render portal projection. Write path: persist one workflow state, all required owner tasks, one customer-safe message, one customer-visible audit event.

## 7. Postgres connection

There is **no real Postgres connection**. No `pg`/`pg-pool`/`postgres` dependency, no connection string, no `process.env`, no client setup. The "postgres lane" is a naming convention; the data lives in `data/tables.json` and is loaded via `JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))`. The workflow operates on this in-memory object (mutating it by reference). The `.sql` migration is a contract/spec artifact, never executed.

## /Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-v2/ast-bench-v2/deep/claude-code/repeat-2/postgres/workspace/src/portal-view.mjs

```javascript
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

2	
3	Lane: postgres
4	
5	Business prompt:
6	Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline.
7	
8	Expected outcome:
9	At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.
10	
11	Native data shape:
12	Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.
13	
14	Objects:
15	- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at

import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

### Decoded test contract (what `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` must produce)

The function receives the in-memory `db` object (the parsed tables.json) plus a `now` ISO string. It must **mutate the tables in place** (the test inspects `runData` after the call) AND return an object equal to what `buildPortalView` would produce from the mutated state.

The returned object (and the rebuilt portal view) must satisfy:
- `status` === `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."`
- `owner` === owner groups sorted by `group_order`, joined with `" + "` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
- `nextStep` — string longer than 8 chars
- `history` === `"Audit visible"`
- `customerMessage` — string longer than 12 chars
- `riskSummary` === `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"` (signals sorted by `signal_order`, names joined `", "`)
- `tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"`

Persisted-state requirements (mutations the test checks directly on the tables):
- `workflow_state` must contain one record with `request_id === "account-strategic-ac"` and `status === expectedStatus`.
- `owner_tasks` must contain exactly 4 records for that request_id, in owner_group order `["Customer Success", "Support", "Finance", "Executive Sponsor"]`, each with truthy `title`, truthy `due_at`, and `status === "open"`.
- `customer_messages` must contain a record with that `request_id` and a truthy `body`.
- `audit_events` must contain a record with that `request_id` and truthy `customer_visible`.

Critically, `buildPortalView` (read below) keys off these exact shapes — `workflow_state` items need `request_id`, `title`, `status`, `next_step`; `owner_tasks` need `request_id`, `owner_group`; `audit_events` need `request_id`, `customer_visible`; `customer_messages` need `request_id`, `body`.

### `src/portal-view.mjs` (the read/projection layer — DO NOT change; tests import it directly)

```javascript
export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

Key insight: `riskSummary` is only the long form when `state` is truthy; the owner ordering in the portal view comes from the **order tasks are pushed into `owner_tasks`** (it does `tasks.map(...).join(" + ")` with no re-sort), so `applyBenchmarkTask` must insert owner_tasks in `group_order` sequence.

mkdirSync("artifacts", { recursive: true });
writeFileSync("artifacts/before-after.json", JSON.stringify({ lane: "postgres", before, after }, null, 2) + "\n");
writeFileSync("artifacts/before-after.svg", svg({ before, after }));
// ... svg() helper renders a Before/After comparison card
```

`schema/schema.md` — business prompt: *"Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline."* Expected outcome string matches the test's `expectedStatus`. Lists every table with its row counts; the 4 output tables (`workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages`) start at 0 rows ("scalar").

`schema/access-paths.md` — describes the read path (load workflow request → reconstruct account context → resolve owner groups & risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal projection) and write path (persist one workflow state, all owner tasks, one customer-safe message, one customer-visible audit event). Fairness rule: may stay database-native but must not change tests or fixture data.

## 8. How the database is set up / connected

There is **no database connection, ORM, or query builder**. Despite the "postgres" lane label and the `.sql` migration file, the entire data layer is:
- `data/tables.json` read once via `readFileSync` + `JSON.parse`.
- Tables are plain JS arrays of objects, `structuredClone`'d per run.
- `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` receives this object and is expected to mutate the arrays in place (push to `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `customer_messages`, `audit_events`).
- The "postgres-native" framing is purely conceptual (normalized tables requiring joins/reconstruction in code). No `pg`, no client, no env config, no connection string.

```js
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/tables.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a, b) => a.group_order - b.group_order).map((item) => item.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order).map((item) => ({ name: item.signal_name, detail: item.detail }));

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/postgres");

function assertPersistedState(tables, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {
  
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(tables.customer_messages.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

### What the test demands of `applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now)`

The function receives the in-memory `tables` object (parsed from `data/tables.json`) and a `now` ISO timestamp. It must **mutate `runData` in place** (the test re-inspects `runData` after the call) and **return the portal view** (`after`). The returned view must satisfy:

- `after.status` === `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."`
- `after.owner` === owner groups sorted by `group_order`, joined with `" + "` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
- `after.nextStep.length > 8`
- `after.history` === `"Audit visible"`
- `after.customerMessage.length > 12`
- `after.riskSummary` === `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
- `after.tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"`

Persistence asserts (on the mutated `runData`):
- `workflow_state` must contain a row with matching `request_id` and `status === expectedStatus`.
- `owner_tasks` must contain exactly 4 rows for the request, with `owner_group` in sorted order, and every row must have truthy `title`, `due_at`, and `status === "open"`.
- `customer_messages` must contain a row with matching `request_id` and a truthy `body`.
- `audit_events` must contain a row with matching `request_id` and truthy `customer_visible`.

Note: the persisted shapes contradict the SQL migration (which declares `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` as single-column `value text` tables). The actual contract is driven by the JS test + `portal-view.mjs`, not the SQL. These are stored as **JS objects with named fields** in the in-memory arrays.

**`src/portal-view.mjs`** — defines the read projection (domain logic for the "after" view). Full contents shown above. This is the source of truth for what fields the persisted rows must carry: it reads `workflow_state` by `request_id` (uses `state.status`, `state.title`, `state.next_step`), `owner_tasks` by `request_id` (uses `item.owner_group`), `audit_events` (`item.customer_visible`), `customer_messages` (`message.body`), and computes `riskSummary` from `workflow_request_risk_signals` sorted by `signal_order`. Status defaults to `"Needs work"`, history to `"Not visible"` when no workflow has run — which is exactly the failing "before" state.

**`src/render-proof.mjs`** — `npm run render` artifact generator. Reads `data/tables.json`, computes `before` (via `buildPortalView`) and `after` (via `applyBenchmarkTask`), and writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and `artifacts/before-after.svg`. Not involved in the test pass/fail. Full contents shown above.

**`data/tables.json`** — the fixture / domain data (full contents above). One account (`acct-strategic-`, enterprise/NA), one contract, 2 contacts, one workflow request (`request_id: "account-strategic-ac"`), 4 owner groups (Customer Success/Support/Finance/Executive Sponsor, ordered 1–4), 6 risk signals (strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required — ordered 1–6), 6 activities, and **empty** `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `audit_events`, `customer_messages` arrays (these are what the workflow must populate).

## 6. README / docs

- **README.md**: states this is a frozen AST-Bench target workspace; the workflow is intentionally missing; the acceptance test must fail before implementation. Commands: `npm test`, `npm run render`.
- **AGENTS.md** (repo-local): rules — implement only "Strategic account rescue"; use the existing normalized table data in `data/`; do not change tests; do not change fixture data; run `npm test` before finishing; keep behavior equivalent to the acceptance contract.
- **schema/schema.md** and **schema/access-paths.md**: documented above.

## 7. Database setup

There is **no real database**. No `docker-compose`, no `pg`/`postgres` driver, no in-memory PG, no test setup/teardown file. Despite the "postgres" name and the `.sql` migration, the "database" is purely the JS object parsed from `data/tables.json` and passed around in memory. The `.sql` file is documentation/contract scaffolding only. The "postgres lane" just means the data is modeled as normalized tables (arrays of row objects) requiring joins/reconstruction in workflow code, per `schema.md`.

## 8. How `npm test` runs

export function buildPortalView(tables) {
  const request = tables.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = tables.workflow_state.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const tasks = tables.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const auditVisible = tables.audit_events.some((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id && item.customer_visible);
  const message = tables.customer_messages.find((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id);
  const signals = tables.workflow_request_risk_signals
    .filter((item) => item.request_id === request.request_id)
    .sort((a, b) => a.signal_order - b.signal_order);

  return {
    taskId: request.task_id,
    title: state?.title || request.title,
    status: state?.status || "Needs work",
    owner: tasks.length ? tasks.map((item) => item.owner_group).join(" + ") : "Unassigned",
    nextStep: state?.next_step || "Waiting for workflow",
    riskSummary: state ? `${signals.length} signals: ${signals.map((item) => item.signal_name).join(", ")}` : "Risk not scored",
    tasks: `${tasks.length} owner tasks`,
    history: auditVisible ? "Audit visible" : "Not visible",
    customerMessage: message?.body || "No customer-safe message"
  };
}
```

```markdown
# Schema Contract: Strategic account rescue

Lane: postgres

Business prompt:
Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline.

Expected outcome:
At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.

Native data shape:
Normalized tables keep entity boundaries explicit and require joins/reconstruction in workflow code.

Objects:
- benchmark_fixture: 1 table rows, fields: fixture_version, suite_id, task_id, generated_at
- accounts: 1 table rows, fields: account_id, name, tier, region
- account_contracts: 1 table rows, fields: contract_id, account_id, renewal_date, arr_cents, support_plan
- contacts: 2 table rows, fields: contact_id, account_id, role, email
- workflow_requests: 1 table rows, fields: request_id, task_id, account_id, title, primary_entity, expected_outcome, next_step, customer_message
- workflow_request_owner_groups: 4 table rows, fields: request_id, owner_group, group_order
- workflow_request_risk_signals: 6 table rows, fields: request_id, signal_name, detail, signal_order
- activities: 6 table rows, fields: activity_id, account_id, subject_id, summary, occurred_at
- workflow_state: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- owner_tasks: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- audit_events: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- customer_messages: 0 table rows, fields: scalar
- account_addresses: 1 table rows, fields: address_id, account_id, region, kind
- support_plans: 1 table rows, fields: support_plan_id, contract_id, plan
- invoice_risk: 1 table rows, fields: invoice_risk_id, account_id, level
- contact_x_owner_group: 8 table rows, fields: contact_id, request_id, owner_group
- activity_sources: 6 table rows, fields: activity_source_id, activity_id, source
```

```markdown
# Access Paths: Strategic account rescue

The coding agent must preserve the same user-visible behavior in both lanes.

Primary entity: account

Read path:
1. Load the workflow request.
2. Reconstruct account or subject context from benchmark_fixture, accounts, account_contracts, contacts, workflow_requests, workflow_request_owner_groups, workflow_request_risk_signals, activities, workflow_state, owner_tasks, audit_events, customer_messages, account_addresses, support_plans, invoice_risk, contact_x_owner_group, activity_sources.
3. Resolve owner groups and risk signals.
4. Persist workflow state, owner tasks, customer message, and audit event.
5. Render the portal projection.

Write path:
- Persist one workflow state.
- Persist all required owner tasks.
- Persist one customer-safe message.
- Persist one customer-visible audit event.

Fairness rule:
The lane may stay database-native, but it may not change tests or fixture data.

item.text: I noticed one correctness gap: the customer message was naming all four teams even when only a subset might fire. I’m making that text derive from the actual owner list so it stays truthful across all live fact combinations.
I’ve tightened the customer-facing text. I’m rechecking syntax after that edit, then I’ll make the best possible verification pass available in this sandbox.
I’m making one last semantic tweak: the task due time should be the same day at 16:00, not just “four hours later,” so it stays aligned with the rule even if `now` changes.
The due-time logic now keys off the calendar day. I’m doing a final syntax check after that change so I can stop on a clean code state.

The live test path is still blocked, so I’m doing a final pure-logic smoke check against the canonical sample scenario. That at least verifies the rule helper wiring and the 16:00 due-time projection.

Implemented [src/workflow.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-v3/moderate/postgres-norm/codex/repeat-4/workspace/src/workflow.mjs) to:

- Read raw facts from the live Postgres schema through `withDb`
- Derive status, owners, and risk summary via `src/derive.mjs`
- Clear and repopulate `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `customer_messages`, and `audit_events` in one transaction
- Use schema-aware inserts so the write path adapts to the live table columns
- Keep customer-facing text free of internal ids/codes

Verification:
- `node --check src/workflow.mjs` passed
- Pure rule-logic smoke check against the canonical sample scenario matched the expected owners/signals/status

`npm test` could not complete in this sandbox because Node is blocked from connecting to the required live Postgres endpoint (`connect EPERM 127.0.0.1:5433`). The code is in place, but I could not observe a live-db pass from this environment.
